# Extracao: transcription_mp4_1__Handling_Objections_in_The_Greeting

---

Okay, here are a list of things you will hear in the greeting. We call these reactionary defense responses. Don't give them too much value. Hey, I'm just looking. Only have 10 minutes. I'm looking for someone else. Look, you're going to be wasting your time to spend any time with me at all. Really I'm just looking. Let me see your manager. We're not buying today. We're not buying for three months. I'm not buying until I get my tax refund. We're going to wait for an insurance check. See, these are the reactionary defense responses. Okay, I'm going to wait a while until our current contract is over. I'm going to wait until the economy approves. Just leave me your card and leave me alone. I just want to look. Do you have financing? How much money would we have to put down? What do you think our current home would be worth? What do you think our existing contract's worth? Look, I'm really worried about my credit. We're upside down. We have negative equity. We're currently under contract. We already have a similar service. What's your best price? I need that right now. Can you beat this price? How much over cost? Look, I can get the same thing down the street. Have you heard any of these things? That's what you're going to hear if you hadn't heard them already. Okay, here's the trick to handling these objections. Handle them not like objections, but as reactionary defense responses. Know that every one of these things, every one, that whole list that I just gave you suggests that you have a real opportunity in front of you, a client, a customer. Handle them only as reactionary defense responses and handle them as a reaction only, a reactive response first before they validate that it could actually be a real objection. Never let these things affect your attitude. Okay? Always agree to what the customer says or asks for. I'm waiting three months. Excellent. We're waiting for it. You know, I'm looking for another person. Excellent. That's great. It's better to surrender here and close later than to fight people and never be able to close a deal. Know what you're going to hear before you hear them tell you this. You guys have to be prepared. You got to know so it doesn't surprise you. Be prepared to the point that you cannot be caught off guard. Also, keep a notebook to log each of these things in so that you become familiar with the finite list. Most salespeople think it's an infinite list forever and it's not. Look, there's only 12 or 13 or 14 things you're ever going to hear. Be prepared. Know what they're going to say. Write them down in your notebook and get your game on.
